assertions.title: Cancel running query
assertions.summary: While the CBMA Spatial Prior query was running (neurosynth engine), the Cancel button was visible alongside the 'Running...' indicator. Clicking Cancel immediately stopped the query and displayed '⊘ Query cancelled.' — NOT 'WebSocketError'. The fix in commit f1fe82ab is confirmed working.

steps.expected: App loads with engine list in sidebar
steps.observed: App loaded, neurosynth and destrieux engines visible

steps.expected: Engine selected, predicate browser and examples panel appear
steps.observed: Engine selected, predicate browser showed Relations/Functions/Probabilistic groups, Examples panel visible

steps.action: Click 'Load example: CBMA Spatial Prior' in examples panel
steps.expected: Slow multi-rule Datalog query loads into code editor, Run Query button enabled
steps.observed: CBMA Spatial Prior query loaded into CodeMirror editor; Run Query button became enabled

steps.expected: Query submits and shows loading state with Cancel button
steps.observed: Button changed to '◌ Running...' (disabled) and a red '✗ Cancel' button appeared

steps.action: Wait 2 seconds, take screenshot of Cancel button visible
steps.expected: Cancel button is visible and running state confirmed
steps.observed: Screenshot shows '◌ Running...' and '✗ Cancel' button (VAL-EXEC-011-cancel-button-visible.png)

steps.expected: Query is stopped and 'Query cancelled.' message appears

steps.action: Wait 2 seconds, take screenshot of cancelled message
steps.observed: Element with class 'error-display error-display--cancelled' shows text '⊘ Query cancelled.' — no WebSocketError anywhere

evidence.network: WebSocket to /v2/query/neurosynth — cancel closes the connection cleanly, no error event

frictions.description: The 'Load example: CBMA Spatial Prior' button uses an aria-label pattern not matching the visible text exactly, so 'agent-browser find text' timed out. Had to use JavaScript eval to click the button.
frictions.resolution: Used agent-browser eval to find and click the button via textContent match

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-EXEC-011 — Cancel running query now correctly shows 'Query cancelled.' message instead of 'WebSocketError'. Fix confirmed working.
